task: Is tap to change pages disabled on mobile?
why do I still see it on my phone? How do I make sure I'm reregistering the service worker on mobile?

task: I manually unregistered the service worker on my android phone. But I have a samsung. The click on to change pages should only be on desktop, but I wonder if there is something weird with my device that is making the click to change pages to still show up?
user_feedback_text: I manually unregistered the service worker on my android phone. But I have a samsung. The click on to change pages should only be on desktop, but I wonder if there is something weird with my device that is making the click to change pages to still show up?

task: It also appears that we don't always correct restore the last read chapter and page number when re-opening the PWA. Why would that be?
user_feedback_text: It also appears that we don't always correct restore the last read chapter and page number when re-opening the PWA. Why would that be?

task: If I'm on chapter 2, and read to chapter the third page of chapter 3. Then refresh, it auto loads chapter 3, but not to the correct page. It auto loads the start of that chapter.
user_feedback_text: If I'm on chapter 2, and read to chapter the third page of chapter 3. Then refresh, it auto loads chapter 3, but not to the correct page. It auto loads the start of that chapter.

task: That didn't work. And I don't necessarily think it's an async issue, I stay on the 2nd page of the next chapter for a while. Then refresh, and it takes me to that chapter, just not to the right page, it goes back to the start of the chapter.
user_feedback_text: That didn't work. And I don't necessarily think it's an async issue, I stay on the 2nd page of the next chapter for a while. Then refresh, and it takes me to that chapter, just not to the right page, it goes back to the start of the chapter.

task: Does the indexdb/localstorage value persist across the different paths on refresh? Or does each path have its own persistance? Is it domain based, or do the paths share?

So couldn't there just be a single values in local storage? "activeChapter" and "chapterProgress". Never track progress of individual chapters. And instead just have a single pair of values that syncrhonously update as the user reads. Active chapter changes, and chapter progress changes.
user_feedback_text: So couldn't there just be a single values in local storage? "activeChapter" and "chapterProgress". Never track progress of individual chapters. And instead just have a single pair of values that syncrhonously update as the user reads. Active chapter changes, and chapter progress changes.
